title: Fix summary tabs, RB partial supply row, total orders sorting, bold orange design codes, remove image icon

requirements.text: Restore the summary tab value computation logic to match v158 behaviour so that metric values (order counts, weight, quantity, CO orders) update correctly and reflect the appropriate subset of orders when switching between dashboard tabs (Total Orders, Ready, Hallmark, Customer Orders, Karigars).

quotes: The summary tabs are not changing values across tabs. It was working fine with all supply logic behaviour in v158

acceptanceCriteria: Switching to the Ready tab shows summary card values computed only from Ready-status orders.
Switching to the Hallmark tab shows summary card values computed from Hallmark-status orders.
Switching to the Total Orders tab shows summary card values for all Pending orders.
Summary card values visibly change when navigating between tabs, matching v158 behaviour.
No regression in supply logic or ghost-row filtering in SummaryCards.tsx.

requirements.text: Fix the RB partial supplied row so it appears in the Ready tab after a partial supply action is performed. When an RB order is partially supplied, the supplied portion must be visible as a row in the Ready tab.

quotes: RB partial supplied row doesn't show yet in the ready tab

acceptanceCriteria: After submitting a partial supply for an RB order via SuppliedQtyDialog, the supplied portion appears as a row in the Ready tab.
The remaining un-supplied portion stays in the Pending/Total Orders tab.
No other rows are affected or hidden in the Ready tab.

requirements.text: Within each design group in the Total Orders tab, sort the rows from longest due (oldest order date) to least due (most recent order date), top to bottom, grouped by weight.

quotes: inside each design group in total orders tab, show data from longest due to least due from top to bottom & weight wise together

acceptanceCriteria: Inside each expanded design group in TotalOrdersTab, rows are sorted so the oldest due date appears at the top and the most recent at the bottom.
Rows with the same weight are grouped together within this sort order.
The sort applies consistently to all design groups.
No other tab ordering is affected.

requirements.text: In the design group header row in the Total Orders tab, display the design code text in bold orange color.

quotes: In design group header where design code is showed, can u display all such design codes in bold orange color

acceptanceCriteria: Every design group header in TotalOrdersTab renders the design code in bold font weight.
The design code text color is orange (matching the app's orange accent, e.g., Tailwind orange-500 or equivalent).
The styling applies to all design group headers, not just selected ones.
No other text in the header is unintentionally changed.

requirements.text: Remove the image icon from the design group header row in the Total Orders tab.

quotes: remove that image icon on the design group in the totals tab

acceptanceCriteria: No image icon is rendered in any design group header row in TotalOrdersTab.
The rest of the header layout (design code, counts, expand/collapse control) remains intact.
No other tabs or components have their image icons removed.

constraints: Only modify code directly related to the 5 specified fixes. All other app behaviour, logic, and UI must remain exactly as-is.
Do not alter backend/main.mo or backend/migration.mo unless strictly required by one of the 5 fixes.
Do not modify any immutable frontend paths: useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, or components/ui.
The draft deployment must not fail — ensure no TypeScript errors, import issues, or build-breaking changes are introduced.
nonGoals: Any new features or UI changes beyond the 5 specified fixes.
Changes to the Hallmark, Customer Orders, Karigars, or Ready tab layouts beyond what is needed for REQ-1 and REQ-2.
Backend schema or data model changes.
Changes to export utilities, barcode scanning, tag printing, or any other page not involved in the fixes.
